review_text: I thought you would give me cashback like 200 rs for transaction above 1000 rs but you didn't give me any of the rewards, so it is better to uninstall you.

The USD conversion via ICICI Bank is misleading. The exchange rate shown in the INDMoney app is not the rate actually applied—there’s a difference of about ₹0.30 per dollar, with the bank charging a higher rate. When I contacted ICICI Bank, they stated that since this is a third-party transaction, they cannot address the discrepancy. On the other hand, INDMoney’s support team was unable to provide a clear explanation for the difference in exchange rates.

Excellent functionality and integration. However, the following key functionality is missing in the portfolio section: It does not show YTD gain and CAGR of stocks purchased from other brokers. INDMoney has information about amount and current price from the depository so letting users manually add purchase date and buy price would enable this feature.

Unreasonably high MTF charges compared to competitors. INDmoney charges 0.1% uncapped brokerage on MTF trades. On a ₹7.7L trade, I was charged around ₹775 for a single order. plus 1k of additional charges (approx)Meanwhile, platforms like Zerodha cap brokerage at around ₹20 per order while offering similar or better margin benefits. If any one are planning to use MTF, think twice. This pricing model can quietly eat into your capital. these guys are looting like anything

I have sent INDmoney a feature request to improve a certain US stocks UX.. they implemented that in just 1 week! I think I can trust this app. I am a Mobile developer myself, I keep USB debugging ON because I need it for my work, but INDmoney being a payment app doesnt unnecessarily force logout me for disabling USBdebugging, they simply stop me when making UPI payments.. this is such a clever and impressive attention to detail! Seems like this app is built by devs who care about their users UX!

I had deposited only ₹100 purely to understand the trading charges on your app. As part of this, I purchased 2 shares at ₹40 each and sold them after 2 days at ₹42 each. This was a very small and straightforward transaction intended only for testing purposes. However, I was shocked to see that ₹2 was deducted as INDmoney charges and an additional ₹22.19 was charged under Government Charges and Taxes.This brings the total deduction to ₹24.19 on a transaction of just ₹80,approx 30%, totally unfair

This application engages in misleading practices by transferring investors’ external mutual fund holdings-originally managed through other distributors (Kuvera / Groww) - into its own demat account without obtaining proper consent. Furthermore, it is now refusing to facilitate the transfer of these holdings back to the Kuvera platform SOA mode from its own demat account. Pathetic and non cooperative attitude of the organisation.
